title: Bruins fans will need to wait a little longer for James Hagens’s NHL debut
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens will not make his NHL debut against the Tampa Bay Lightning, Boston's coach Marco Sturm announced.
Marco Sturm emphasized patience with Hagens, citing the need for him to acclimate to the team's system.
Hagens practiced on a 'fifth' line and earned some reps on the second power-play unit in recent sessions.

title: Boston billionaires, historic figures land on Forbes’ ‘self-made’ lists
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Forbes' list of 'The Greatest Self-Made Americans' features Boston-area billionaires Noubar Afeyan, Bill Cummings, and Herb Chambers.
Historic self-made Americans with Boston ties include Benjamin Franklin and Sheldon Adelson on Forbes' related historic list.
The rankings assess financial success, obstacles overcome, and long-term impact with 'Self-Made Scores' of nine or ten required.

title: What you need to know about the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The 130th Boston Marathon on April 20, 2026, features 30,000 runners from over 130 countries and all 50 U.S. states.
The marathon introduces six start waves this year, with The Boston Athletic Association organizing the iconic event.
Spectators can watch live coverage on WCVB Channel 5 with streaming on the Very Local app starting at 4 a.m.

title: Patriots reportedly adding former Titans assistant Shane Bowen to coaching staff
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Shane Bowen reportedly joins the New England Patriots as an analyst, after previous roles with the Giants and Titans.
Bowen and Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel have a long history, having worked together at Ohio State, Texans, and Titans.
Patriots' defensive coordinator Zak Kuhr sees Bowen as a mentor, continuing an offseason reshaping of the defensive staff.

title: Where are the public restrooms in Boston?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston offers public restrooms at libraries, community centers, and some restaurants and firehouses, supported by an interactive city map.
Massachusetts law allows those with medical conditions to access employee-only restrooms when public options are unavailable.
Restroom access became crucial during the pandemic, highlighting their role as essential infrastructure in public spaces.

title: Masconomet school officials did not adequately respond to harassment claims, student athlete says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Charlotte Rollins, a freshman at Masconomet Regional High School, alleges inadequate response to harassment claims by school officials.
Rollins claims she faced bullying as the only girl on the varsity golf team and received threats of sexual violence.
Masconomet teachers confirmed discrimination issues, urging the School Committee to intervene and address the lack of accountability.

title: Report: Mass. drivers have paid more than $1 billion extra at the pump since the Iran war began
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts drivers paid over $137 billion more for gasoline since the Iran War began, with gas prices exceeding $4 per gallon.
The Joint Economic Committee reports a 35% increase in gas prices, raising costs by $37-$59 per fill-up based on vehicle type.
Heating oil costs in Massachusetts rose 37.9% from March 2025, adding $224 per tank for average families.

title: Mass. campgrounds still have availability for World Cup fans
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Massachusetts campgrounds offer availability for soccer fans attending World Cup matches at Boston Stadium in Foxborough.
Canoe River Campground and Jellystone Park Cranberry Acres provide camping options near Boston Stadium during matches.
Boston hotels face increased demand and pricing during World Cup; options include camping as an economical alternative.

title: This stroke survivor is running Boston for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Tim Rafferty, 43, a stroke survivor, is running the Boston Marathon to support Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
In 2019, Rafferty suffered a hemorrhagic stroke that left him initially paralyzed on his left side.
Rafferty credits Spaulding Hospital with helping him regain mobility and run the marathon post-recovery.

title: ‘I appreciated that’: Jayson Tatum credited Knicks fans’ applause in MSG return
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to Madison Square Garden, receiving applause from Knicks fans despite Boston's 112-106 loss.
Tatum played 40 minutes, marking his return to the site of his Achilles injury in the 2025 playoffs.
Tatum expressed gratitude for New York fans' respect, a rare acknowledgment for a Celtics player in MSG.
